summary: Post-send bookkeeping (resolve_alert/stamp_success) runs INSIDE the try after delivery; if it raises, the except releases the won claim and the slot re-fires -> duplicate briefing
failure_scenario: claim_slot wins (206) and send_now POSTs the briefing. Then resolve_alert (335)/stamp_success (336) open fresh sqlite connections against a DB being concurrently written by ~10 workers + heartbeat + UV monitor with no busy_timeout override and default rollback journal -> 'database is locked' OperationalError is realistic. Caught by broad except at 345 with claimed=True, so release_claim (352) DELETEs the sent_log row. User already got the briefing, but the slot is re-fireable: catch-up (same local_date, within grace) or a restart sees was_sent()==False and delivers the SAME briefing again. Silent double-send.
evidence: from sweep pass (unverified)

summary: A bare location-taking command (`!weather` with no arg) never fetches, so the handler receives result=None and crashes -> generic error reply; default-location feature is dead on Discord
failure_scenario: Bare `!weather`/`!sun`/`!wind`/`!alerts`/`!uv`/`!next-cloudy` -> parse_command arg=None, needs_flags=False. dispatch_spec guard `if arg is not None or spec.needs_flags:` (dispatch.py:76) is False, so cache.lookup is SKIPPED and result stays None. The bind closure calls weather_views.weather(None) -> result.forecast -> AttributeError, caught by on_message envelope -> generic 'something went wrong'. The documented default-location behavior (CLI's run_weather does resolve_location(None)) is broken over Discord. Root cause: module has only a needs_flags signal, not a takes_location signal.
evidence: from sweep pass (unverified)

summary: Catch-up scan runs during build_runtime BEFORE the ReadyGate health gate, so missed slots fire before API key/network is confirmed and are never re-attempted
merged_from: wiring.py:197 (PLAUSIBLE low, lifecycle-ordering — startup catch-up before gate/SIGTERM handler)
failure_scenario: On a reboot where the OpenWeather key is still propagating (the '~2h key activation' case the gate exists for) or the network is briefly down at boot, plan_catchup's fire_slot fetches, gets a 401/timeout, exhausts its two-burst retry, releases the claim, and fires a failure alert for EVERY missed slot — all before ready_gate.run() (daemon.py:1465) executes. When the gate later passes, catch-up does not run again (it ran once inside build_runtime at wiring.py:197), so every recoverable missed briefing is permanently lost, replaced by an alert. Also catch-up fires before the SIGTERM handler is installed (daemon.py:1418) so a stop during a mid-pause is a non-graceful kill.
evidence: Ordering CONFIRMED: run_daemon calls build_runtime (daemon.py:1389) -> _run_catchup (wiring.py:197) which calls fire_slot(late=True) for each missed slot; SIGTERM handler installed at 1418 and ready_gate.run at 1465, both strictly AFTER catch-up. mid_pause default 2700s (models.py:282). Sweep high verdict on the un-gated recovery loss is unverified.

summary: sleep=stop_event.wait does NOT abandon the schedule on shutdown — tenacity discards wait()'s return, so SIGTERM drains all remaining ~16 attempts back-to-back at zero delay
merged_from: HUB retry.py:241 (SWEEP high, root cause in hub)
failure_scenario: fire_slot wraps send_now in build_retrying with sleep=stop_event.wait (retry.py:241). On SIGTERM the daemon sets stop mid-retry. tenacity does self.sleep(do) and IGNORES the boolean return (verified against installed tenacity __init__.py 443/480); Event.wait returns True immediately when set but tenacity proceeds to the next attempt and every subsequent pause is also zero-length, so the daemon fires the full remaining burst of send_now attempts (up to ~15) back-to-back during shutdown — a rapid volley of OpenWeather fetches / Discord POSTs. Contradicts the D-07/Pitfall-1 interruptibility guarantee that 'the entire schedule abandons the instant the Event is set.' Fix: a wait wrapper that raises/stops when the event returns True. ROUTES UPSTREAM (hub).
evidence: from sweep pass (unverified); root is hub retry.py:241, consumer symptom at daemon.py:235

summary: Daemon `run` startup loads config via load_config only — never runs assert_unique_names or validate_config_and_templates that check-config and reload both enforce; duplicate names/ids and bad templates boot green
merged_from: wiring.py:167 (SWEEP high, build_runtime registers without validation)
failure_scenario: Two [[locations]] with DIFFERENT names but the SAME explicit id: both register distinct jobs (job id keyed on name, daemon.py:631) and both fire, but each fire claims on location.id (daemon.py:206) against UNIQUE(location_name,send_time,local_date); first claim wins, second logs 'slot skipped (already sent)' and never delivers — a silently missed briefing every morning. Same startup gap also lets a typo'd template {placeholder} or missing template file boot green (check-config/reload would reject); at 9am fire_slot->validate_template/load_template raises, is swallowed, and the briefing is silently missed daily. The three entry points (startup / check-config / reload) disagree on what a valid config is.
evidence: cli.py:986 _load_config_reporting -> load_config (loader.py:24-27) does only tomllib.load + model_validate — no assert_unique_names, no template validation. build_runtime (wiring.py:108/167) calls _register_jobs directly; assert_unique_names/validate_config_and_templates only wired into ReloadEngine validate (wiring.py:233) i.e. reload-only, and into selfcheck/check. Duplicate-id silent-drop reproduced. (Casefold name-collision does NOT drop due to BINARY collation but IS a validation gap.)

category: selfcheck false classification (permanent error masked as transient)
summary: Permanent config/template/location errors (and empty-locations) are classified NETWORK_NOT_READY, so the daemon warn-loops forever, never sends, and never surfaces a hard failure
merged_from: selfcheck.py:80 (SWEEP low, empty-locations same family via distinct path)
failure_scenario: A bad template placeholder (validate_template raises), a duplicate location name (assert_unique_names), an unresolvable location (resolve_location), or an empty [[locations]] list (line 81 ValueError) all fall into the bare `except Exception` (116-122) and return reason=NETWORK_NOT_READY. to_health_result maps that to Severity.WARNING (only AUTH_FAILED -> CRITICAL), and ReadyGate re-probes on stop.wait(120s) forever — never emitting READY=1, never starting the scheduler, never firing a briefing, never a critical alert. Since the daemon `run` path never validates these (see F05), a duplicate name / typo'd template / empty locations passes startup and produces a permanent silent WARNING loop: the bot looks 'alive but not ready' and silently sends zero briefings.
evidence: selfcheck.py:116-122 catches ALL non-HTTP exceptions -> NETWORK_NOT_READY. run_self_check calls validate_template(84), assert_unique_names(87), resolve_location(91), and raises on empty locations(81) — all caught here. to_health_result(139) maps NETWORK_NOT_READY->WARNING. ReadyGate.run loops while not stop, returns True only on result.ok, re-probes every 120s. Reachable because daemon run loads via load_config which skips these validators.

summary: One-time Discord online-ping moved to BEFORE the systemd READY=1 emit, so a slow/hung webhook delays readiness and systemd can kill the still-starting service
failure_scenario: Original emit_online (daemon.py:1179-1189) fired notifier.ready() (READY=1) at step 4 BEFORE channel.send(ping) at step 5. In the refactor the ping runs INSIDE _on_online (wiring.py:305-313) and ReadyGate.run calls notifier.ready() only AFTER _on_online returns (ready_gate.py:96 then :98). A slow/hanging Discord webhook POST at startup now blocks READY=1; if it exceeds TimeoutStartSec systemd treats the service as failed and kills/restarts it — opposite of the module's claim that READY is strictly after scheduler.start(). Fix: emit ping after notifier.ready(), not before.
evidence: from sweep pass (unverified)

category: send-failure not detected / HTTP status not checked
summary: fire_forecast_slot ignores the DeliveryResult from channel.send(), so a Discord non-2xx delivery failure counts as success and resets the failure streak
failure_scenario: A scheduled forecast slot posts and Discord returns non-2xx (400/413/429-exhausted). _post maps to DeliveryResult(ok=False) and NEVER raises, so control falls through to _note_forecast_success() which RESETS the streak. The forecast is never delivered, yet _note_forecast_failure / the WR-05 dead-slot CRITICAL log + operator alert never fire (they only run in the except branch). A chronically-dead forecast slot failing via ok=False stays completely silent — violating 'retry then alert rather than silently miss'. Sibling fire_slot DOES inspect result.ok; this path does not.

summary: load_settings() is called unguarded in every command path; a missing/malformed env secret crashes with a raw pydantic traceback, violating the CLI's clean-failure contract

summary: 'READ-ONLY' functions take a write lock every call — _SCHEMA runs INSERT OR IGNORE on every connect, so a status read during a daemon write can raise 'database is locked'
failure_scenario: read_heartbeat/read_health/was_sent/claimed_uv_kinds all call conn.executescript(_SCHEMA) on connect, and _SCHEMA ends with two INSERT OR IGNORE INTO heartbeat/health. executescript force-commits and the INSERTs acquire a RESERVED/write lock, so a 'status' read fired while the daemon is mid-write can raise sqlite3.OperationalError: database is locked after the default 5s busy timeout — a status query can crash even though docstrings claim 'READ-ONLY: writes nothing'.
evidence: from sweep pass (unverified)

summary: high/low_display falls back to current temp when only the METRIC daily field is missing, discarding a valid imperial high

The day's real high is silently lost even though it was fetched in one unit.
evidence: from sweep pass (unverified)

summary: raise_for_status() raises an HTTPStatusError whose message embeds the full URL including appid=<key>, at both the onecall (67) and geocode (84) call sites — defeating the Pitfall-6 key-redaction claim
merged_from: client.py:67 (CONFIRMED high, onecall + Discord inbound leak path)
client.py:84 (SWEEP high, geocode call site same leak)
failure_scenario: On a 401/403 (documented 'subscription not active / key not propagated'), fetch_onecall (67) and geocode (84) raise httpx.HTTPStatusError whose str() embeds the secret URL: "...appid=SECRETKEY123&units=imperial". summary: An in-flight fetch that started before invalidate() re-populates the cache with a pre-reload (stale) result, surviving until TTL
failure_scenario: Operator taps a command -> cache miss -> lookup_weather runs off-loop WITHOUT the lock. While in flight, a config hot-reload commits and calls cache.invalidate() (daemon.py:1013/wiring.py:220), clearing the dict. The in-flight fetch then completes and does `self._cache[key] = result` (119), re-inserting a result computed against the OLD config (old lat/lon/units/template). invalidate() has no epoch/generation guard, so the stale entry is served for repeated commands for up to ttl_seconds (~10 min) — defeating the exact guarantee invalidate() exists for. resolve_location keys on Location.id, so if the id survived the rename/coord-change the stale entry masks the reloaded config entirely.
evidence: from sweep pass (unverified)

summary: Catchup only composes TODAY's local date, so a late-evening slot missed across local midnight is never recovered
failure_scenario: A slot at 23:45 fails to fire and the daemon comes back at 00:15 the next local day (30 min late, inside the 90-min GRACE). plan_catchup builds naive=datetime(now_local.year,month,day,23,45) using TODAY's date, so the recomputed instant is ~23.5h in the FUTURE and is skipped as 'not due yet' (line 170 scheduled > now_utc). Yesterday's genuinely-missed 23:45 slot is never a candidate, so that briefing is silently lost even though inside grace.
evidence: catchup.py:155 composes from TODAY only; docstring line 5/136 confirms 'bounded to TODAY's slots'. Correct behavior would also test the prior local day's instant against now_utc-GRACE. Requires the outage window to straddle midnight with a slot within 90 min of it — realistic but narrow.

category: wrong-condition / no hysteresis on instantaneous value
summary: All-clear fires and latches on a single momentary instantaneous UV dip mid-day, permanently ending the protect window while it is still active
failure_scenario: After a crossing is claimed, branch 3 gates only on summary.current (= instantaneous current.uvi). At solar noon a passing cloud makes one 15-min tick read UV 5.8 vs threshold 6.0; branch 3's `current < threshold and 'crossing' in prior and 'allclear' not in prior` is true, so it claims allclear (once/day, durable INSERT OR IGNORE) and posts '✅ UV back below 6 — protect window over.' UV climbs back to 8 minutes later but allclear can never re-open, so the user is told the danger is over while UV is peaking. No hysteresis/window_end/persistence gate exists in branch 3.
evidence: uvmonitor.py:318 gates on summary.current (instantaneous, uv.py:223). claim_uv_alert durable once/day (store.py:379-386). window_end referenced only in _fmt_window (display), never in the decision. Realistic daytime cloud-transient path; wrong safety message, no crash — high not critical.

summary: gate_until_healthy, emit_online, and _do_reload are dead in production after the ReadyGate/ReloadEngine refactor (still exercised only by tests)
failure_scenario: Phase-25 moved gating to ReadyGate (wiring builds _health_check/_on_fail/on_online; run_daemon drives ready_gate.run) and reload to ReloadEngine. No production caller of gate_until_healthy (1108-1156), emit_online (1159-1189), or _do_reload (879-1026) — only doc-comments + tests. They duplicate the live gate/reload logic, inviting a future fix landing in the dead copy.

summary: on_applied side-effect ordering: channel.send happens before cache.invalidate, so a slow/blocking Discord post delays the ForecastCache invalidation the inbound bot relies on
failure_scenario: After a committed reload that changes a location's coordinates, _on_applied first calls channel.send('✅ config reloaded') (a POST that can rate-limit-retry and block) and only THEN calls cache.invalidate() (220). During that window an inbound `!weather <loc>` served from the still-stale ForecastCache returns the OLD coordinates' forecast until invalidate() finally fires. The doc claims 'SAME order as the in-place path' but gates the correctness-relevant invalidate behind a best-effort network call.
evidence: wiring.py:215 channel.send before :220 cache.invalidate. discord.py send->_post->webhook.execute() with rate_limit_retry=True can sleep-retry. ForecastCache keyed on Location.id, 600s TTL; same instance handed to inbound bot. Transient stale read on edge-case concurrence.

summary: on_rejected reload closure omits the stdlib-logger reject line the in-place path emits, so a rejected SIGHUP reload is under-reported in the journal
failure_scenario: The in-place reject path (_do_reload, daemon.py:924-925) logs BOTH _log.error and _stdlog.error('reload rejected: %s') so operators tailing the stdlib/journal stream still see rejections. The wiring on_rejected closure (256) only posts to the channel; the ReloadEngine reject path (reload.py:134) emits only structlog _log.error, never the _stdlog line. On a SIGHUP reload of a malformed config, the journal 'reload rejected' line silently disappears even though the channel post fires.
evidence: from sweep pass (unverified)

summary: value_close prewarn fires with no rising/future-crossing guard — both when UV never reaches threshold today (stays_below) and when UV has already peaked and is descending
failure_scenario: Branch 2's value_close = (threshold - current) <= margin has no `not stays_below` and no rising/future-crossing guard (unlike time_close). (a) On a day peaking just under threshold (max 5.5 vs 6, current within margin), a 'UV nearing 6 — sunscreen soon' prewarn posts but the promised crossing never comes and no crossing/all-clear follows. (b) Late afternoon after UV already peaked above threshold and is now descending but still within margin (threshold 6, current 5.2), if no prewarn/crossing was claimed (bot started after the peak), value_close triggers a 'sunscreen soon' prewarn even though the protective window is effectively over and UV is falling.
evidence: from sweep pass (unverified)

category: never-fire gap between crossing and pre-warn branches
summary: If UV already crossed up earlier while the bot was down and is now below threshold, no branch claims `crossing`, so all-clear can never fire — the day is silently skipped
failure_scenario: Bot's first successful tick of the day lands in the afternoon (e.g. 21:30, in daylight, sunset 23:40) after the day's up-cross already elapsed, with current now well below threshold. Branch 1 needs current>=threshold (false); branch 2's time_close needs a FUTURE crossing (delta negative -> false) and value_close may be false; branch 3 needs 'crossing' in prior (never claimed). The monitor is entirely silent for a day that genuinely had a high-UV window, and can never emit an all-clear because no crossing row exists.
evidence: Reproduced: compute_uv(current=3.0,threshold=6.0) crossing_time past, stays_below False; _decide(margin=1.0) -> sent=[] claimed=set(). No test covers this (all all-clear tests presuppose a claimed crossing). Requires afternoon-first-tick after crossing elapsed AND current dropped >value_margin below threshold.

summary: daily0-date-mismatch skip also suppresses the daylight-independent all-clear, stranding the day's lifecycle
failure_scenario: Near a tz/DST/midnight boundary the payload's daily[0] resolves to the prior calendar day, so _daily0_matches_today returns False and _evaluate_location returns True at line 167 BEFORE computing prior/all-clear. If a `crossing` was already claimed earlier today, WR-01 requires the all-clear to still close the day out, but this early-return blocks it while daily[0] is stale — the all-clear may be silently skipped for the day. Same structural gap as the line-154 None-sun skip (F35), distinct trigger (present-but-wrong-date daily[0]).
evidence: from sweep pass (unverified)

summary: SelectedContext is seeded once at wiring and never reconciled on hot-reload; a renamed/removed selected location leaves a stale value the dropdown no longer shows as selected
failure_scenario: Config hot-reload renames locations[0] (the seeded default) 'Home'->'HomeCity'. cache.invalidate() clears the forecast cache but nothing resets `selection`, which still holds 'Home'. On next panel render LocationSelect builds options with default=(n=='Home') matching nothing, so the dropdown shows the empty 'Location' placeholder. If the operator taps Weather/UV/a forecast button without re-selecting, _dispatch passes selection.value='Home' to resolve_location -> UnknownLocationError surfaced for a location they never see selected.
evidence: wiring.py:452 SelectedContext(locations[0]) never reset in the reload path. panel.py re-derives the option LIST live (293) but default flag uses stale value (226). Needs a rename/remove of the selected location AND an operator tapping without noticing the reset; a re-select clears it.

summary: Select/command callback re-render crashes inside _safe_error_edit when config was hot-reloaded to zero locations, leaving the panel stuck on the disabled cue
failure_scenario: If a hot-reload leaves config.locations empty (or holder.current() transiently raises) between renders, edit_message(view=panel._build_clone_view()) raises because _select_contributor fail-loud raises ValueError on empty locations (panel.py:296-300). The except branch calls _safe_error_edit, which ALSO calls _build_clone_view() (panelkit.py:448) -> same ValueError -> swallowed -> response already done -> only a log. The panel is frozen showing the old view with no user-visible recovery. The on_command path has the identical latent failure.

summary: LocationSelect.callback mutates the shared selection BEFORE acking, so a failed/expired edit_message leaves selection changed with no visible re-render
failure_scenario: Operator changes the dropdown after the interaction token expired (>3s dispatch stall or a rapid re-selection with a stale token). self._selection.set(self.values[0]) (248) commits the new location, then interaction.response.edit_message (252) raises; control falls to _safe_error_edit which posts the generic error and re-renders a clone reflecting the NEW selection. The user sees an error but the internal selection silently advanced to a value they cannot tell was applied — subsequent command buttons act on the just-set location while the UI implied failure.
evidence: panel.py:248 set() before ack at :252; SelectedContext.set unconditionally rebinds with no rollback; command buttons read ctx.value. Requires ack to fail on a fresh 3s token — edge case on a single-user bot; recoverable by re-selecting.

summary: Bare '+' or '-' flag token yields _day_token('') -> ValueError, surfacing as the generic error reply instead of the actionable flag message
failure_scenario: Operator sends `!weekday-forecast home -` (or a lone `+`). folded=='-' hits the startswith('-') branch; lstrip('-')=='' (also '+'[1:]==''); _day_token('') raises ValueError('unknown day flag ""...'). This bubbles out of dispatch_spec; bot.py only special-cases UnknownLocationError, so it hits the outer non-propagating except -> operator gets opaque 'Sorry — something went wrong fetching that.' instead of the intended fail-loud 'use one of [...]' guidance.
evidence: Reproduced: `unknown day flag '': use one of [...]`. bot.py:500 only catches UnknownLocationError; generic ValueError -> outer except (506) -> _ERROR_REPLY. Malformed-flag edge case, no crash.

summary: A long-form `--sat`/`--mon` day flag is only ever treated as a DROP, never an ADD, because `--` matches the elif-'-' branch before add is considered
failure_scenario: Operator sends `!weekday-forecast home --sat` expecting parity with `+sat` (the '--compact'/'--detailed' long forms are supported, implying '--' long forms are accepted). folded=='--sat' does not match compact/detailed literals, is not caught by startswith('+'), and falls into startswith('-') -> lstrip('-')=='sat' -> added to DROP not ADD. The day the operator asked to ADD is silently dropped — wrong forecast, no error.
evidence: Reproduced: parse_forecast_flags('home --sat') -> add=set(), drop={'sat'}. --compact/--detailed are documented/tested equivalents of +compact/+detailed, so a user extrapolating '--X == +X' gets a silent DROP. Undocumented/edge input spelling.

summary: Inbound `!weather <loc>` replies never render the 📍 location indicator because render_embed is called with no location=, diverging from the panel which always passes it
failure_scenario: `!weather London` -> on_message calls render_embed(reply) with NO location kwarg (bot.py:504), so render_embed's `location is not None` guard (219) is False and the 📍 indicator is suppressed even though the user named a location. The panel path routes through _render_bridge (wiring.py:392) which passes location=render_arg, so the SAME CommandReply shows 📍 on the panel but not on the direct command reply — the two surfaces meant to render field-for-field identically (D-07 parity) drift on the location header.

failure_scenario: Any !weekday / !weekend forecast (common path). render_embed (bot.py:224) sets the embed title to reply.title='Weekday forecast — New York', and reply.text begins with the template's own header '📅 Weekday forecast — New York'. Discord shows the title AND again as the first body line; render_text (cli.py:598-602) prints reply.title then the body, duplicating on the CLI too. Frozen into the golden snapshot. summary: next_cloudy near-term loop silently drops every night-time cloudy hour, so a fully nocturnal cloudy stretch is never reported and falls through to daily days 3-8
failure_scenario: User asks !next-cloudy at threshold 60 while tonight is fully overcast (clouds>=60 only during 22:00-05:00). The hourly loop returns only on a DAYTIME hour (line 233 _is_daytime gate); every qualifying overnight bucket passes the threshold check but fails _is_daytime and is skipped; the daily fallback explicitly skips today/tomorrow (daily[2:], line 244), so the reply reports a day-3+ cloudy day or 'No cloudy day', hiding the imminent overnight cloudy period.
